original_title: Trellix Confirms Source Code Breach With Unauthorized Repository Access
selection_reasoning: A security company being breached represents the ultimate irony and offers lessons about how even the watchers themselves are vulnerable. The vague disclosure adds another layer of narrative tension about transparency.

field_note.title: When the Watchers Are Watched
field_note.subtitle: The Guardian's Greatest Vulnerability
field_note.excerpt: Those who make their living watching others often forget they too cast shadows.
field_note.epigraph: The guardian who believes themselves beyond the reach of thieves has already lost their most precious secrets.
body_paragraphs: Among the great watchtowers of the digital realm, few names command as much respect as those who have dedicated themselves to the craft of protection. These are the guild masters, the ones who forge the very tools and enchantments that other kingdoms use to defend against corruption. Their expertise is their currency, their methods their most guarded treasure.

Yet even these masters of defense are not immune to the very threats they spend their lives studying. Word has reached us that one such guild's most sacred vaults-the repositories where they craft their protective spells-have been breached. The intruders made away with portions of the source knowledge, the very blueprints of defense.

What makes this breach particularly telling is not just the target, but the guardian's response. Speaking in riddles and half-truths, they acknowledged the theft while revealing little of its true scope. Such measured words often mask deeper wounds than bold proclamations of minor scratches.

This incident serves as a humbling reminder that expertise in defense does not grant immunity from attack. Indeed, those who make their living protecting others often become the most attractive targets, for their secrets are worth more than gold to those who would do harm. The watchers, too, must be watched.
